findings.evidence: `rg -n "p-torsion|aᵖ"` hits lines 22-23, 26, 39-41, 55-57, 157, 259-260; the actual public theorem at lines 261-262 states `a ∈ pointsHom.range ↔ (Multiplicative.toAdd a) ^ p = 0`.
findings.fix: Replace these occurrences with “Frobenius kernel”, “additive `p`-nilpotent points”, or “elements `a` with `a^p = 0`”, and avoid saying this is the additive group’s `p`-torsion subgroup.
findings.issue: The docs call the represented points “the `p`-torsion of the additive group” in the module docstring, main-declaration list, `hopfIdeal`, and `mem_range_pointsHom_iff`, but additive `p`-torsion means killed by multiplication by `p`, while the theorem proves the Frobenius-kernel/nilpotence condition `a^p = 0`.

summary: The added documentation repeatedly identifies `αₚ(A)` with additive `p`-torsion, but the formal statement characterizes Frobenius-kernel points `a^p = 0`; this is a materially misleading description of the represented functor.
